mechanism_narrative: Positive 3-month growth in PAYEMS signals expanding household income and business hiring momentum, which raises expected earnings breadth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if higher 3-month PAYEMS growth does not produce higher 20-day S&P 500 returns, with walk-forward Rank IC below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A FEDFUNDS crossing above 2.25% tightens the discount-rate channel and reduces equity valuation support, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations with FEDFUNDS above 2.25% produce positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of walk-forward cases.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL rises while FEDFUNDS rises, inflation pressure forces tighter policy and raises discount rates faster than earnings expectations adjust, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint CPIAUCSL-up and FEDFUNDS-up state has nonnegative average 60-day excess S&P 500 returns or loses significance at p > 0.05 in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Strong 3-month growth in RSAFS indicates accelerating consumer demand that lifts near-term revenue expectations for index constituents, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if RSAFS growth above 1.0% is followed by lower average 20-day S&P 500 returns than non-triggered periods in two consecutive 3-year walk-forward windows.

mechanism_narrative: HOUST below 1300 thousand units signals weakening housing-sensitive demand and tighter conditions for cyclicals, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST below 1300 is followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of triggered observations or has Rank IC above -0.03.

mechanism_narrative: Rising INDPRO over the prior 3 months signals improving real production and operating leverage, which raises near-term earnings revisions and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if positive 3-month INDPRO changes fail to predict positive 5-day S&P 500 returns, with hit rate at or below 50% over two consecutive walk-forward years.

mechanism_narrative: A UNRATE rise greater than 0.3 percentage points over 6 months signals labor-market deterioration and recession-risk repricing, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE 6-month change crosses above 0.3 percentage points
failure_condition: Reject if triggered UNRATE increases are not followed by negative average 60-day S&P 500 returns or if the downside hit rate is below 55% in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE is below 4% and FEDFUNDS is above 2%, full-employment tightening dominates growth support and compresses equity multiples, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint UNRATE below 4% and FEDFUNDS above 2% state produces positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of triggered walk-forward cases.
